title: Ayotzinapa Tracker Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: March 2026 brought significant developments in the Ayotzinapa case. A federal court ordered Mexico's Defense Ministry to deliver 853 military intelligence files from the night of the disappearance, though President Sheinbaum publicly disputed the order. The Sheinbaum government separately sought UN backing and invited former GIEI experts Ángela Buitrago and Carlos Martín Beristain to return — while advancing a new phone-record analysis strategy that yielded 10+ arrests in November 2025. On the 138-month anniversary (March 26), normalistas marched in Chilpancingo demanding concrete results. A new contested claim was added covering the military's continued resistance to full file disclosure.

title: Ayotzinapa Tracker Update — Thu Mar 26, 2026
summary: 138-Month Anniversary: Normalistas March in Chilpancingo Demanding Truth.

title: Ayotzinapa Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum formally requested the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights to propose international specialists to evaluate Mexico's Ayotzinapa investigation.

title: Ayotzinapa Tracker Update — Mon Mar 9, 2026
summary: Former GIEI members Ángela Buitrago and Carlos Martín Beristain confirmed they are awaiting a formal response from the Sheinbaum government regarding reintegrating into the Ayotzinapa investigation.

title: Ayotzinapa Tracker Update — Wed Mar 4, 2026
summary: A federal court ordered Mexico's Defense Ministry (SEDENA) to deliver 853 military intelligence dossiers related to the Ayotzinapa case.
